commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can align the lint namespace guard with the existing repair guard, but maintainers still need final compatibility/security-boundary approval afterward.; Needs attention: The diff has no supply-chain changes, but the lint namespace gap is a concrete security-boundary concern because plugin-controlled checks can appear under core doctor IDs in lint output.; Review finding: [P2] Reject... commands.review_summary:
This PR moves core doctor health checks into ordered doctor contributions, appends non-core registry checks after core checks for `doctor --lint`, preserves legacy repair ownership, removes the conversion-plan ledger, and updates doctor docs/tests.

PR surface: Source -84, Tests +546, Docs +2. Total +464 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: a plugin-kind check with an unused `core/doctor/*` ID is not rejected by `doctor --lint` because the lint guard only checks exact current core IDs. I did not run tests because this review is read-only and the checkout must remain clean.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can align the lint namespace guard with the existing repair guard, but maintainers still need final compatibility/security-boundary approval afterward.
